messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: agents window -- Losing model picker in local agent host
ISSUE BODY: see attached.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e0e5e9de-10ef-4ac2-a768-3c4c76afdc59

--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Identity  
`LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider`:  
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| `id` | `'local-agent-host'` (`LOCAL_AGENT_HOST_PROVIDER_ID`) |
| `label` | `"Local Agent Host"` |
| `icon` | `Codicon.vm` |
| `supportsLocalWorkspaces` | `true` |
| `browseActions` | `[]` (local folders are browsed through the shared workspace picker) |
| `order` | `-1` when `chat.agentHost.defaultSessionsProvider` is enabled (sorts before all other providers), else `1` |
| `sessionTypes` | Dynamically populated from the local agent host's `rootState.agents`; the type label is the agent's unadorned `displayName` (e.g. `"Copilot"`), the type **id** is the agent provider name (e.g. `copilotcli`) so the same agent shares one session type across local and remote hosts |  
When the default-provider setting flips, the provider re-fires `onDidChangeSessionTypes` so the management service re-collects and re-sorts session types with the new `order`.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### Model Picker  
The model picker is no longer contributed per provider. The sessions core contributes a single `ModelPicker` (`contrib/chat/browser/modelPicker.ts`) into `Menus.NewSessionConfig` that wraps the shared workbench `ModelPickerActionItem`. It reads the available models from the active session's provider via `ISessionsProvider.getModels(sessionId)`, the picker presentation options via `ISessionsProvider.getModelPickerOptions(sessionId)`, remembers the last used model per provider per session type, and applies the selection through `ISessionsProvider.setModel(sessionId, modelId)`.  
This provider returns models from `getModels` based on the active session:
- **CLI / Claude** sessions return registered language models whose `targetChatSessionType` matches the session type.
- **Cloud** sessions synthesize `ILanguageModelChatMetadataAndIdentifier` entries from the extension-host `models` option group; `setModel` additionally persists the choice as the option-group value so the extension host honours it.  
`getModelPickerOptions` returns grouped models with featured models shown and no "Manage Models" action (that action is offered only by the local provider).

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Tests  
`test/browser/` covers the provider and its pickers: `localAgentHostSessionsProvider.test.ts`, `agentHostAgentPicker.test.ts`, `agentHostAgents.test.ts`, `agentHostModelPicker.test.ts`, `agentHostClaudePermissionModePicker.test.ts`, `agentHostSkillButtons.test.ts`, `agentSessionSettingsFileSystemProvider.test.ts`, `openSessionEventsFile.test.ts`, and `agentHost/agentHostPermissionPickerDelegate.test.ts`.  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\src\vs\sessions\contrib\providers\copilotChatSessions\COPILOT_CHAT_SESSIONS_PROVIDER.md =====

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix model picker disappearing in Local Agent Host sessions",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a VS Code AI Chat user, I want the model picker to remain visible and functional when using Local Agent Host sessions so that I can continue my conversation without losing context or having to re-select models.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a Local Agent Host session is active, When opening the model picker via Ctrl+M or menu, Then the picker displays all available models from the agent host",
    "Given a model was previously selected in a Local Agent Host session, When reopening that same session, Then the previously selected model remains highlighted/selected",
    "Given the model picker is open for a Local Agent Host session, When selecting a different model and closing the picker, Then the selection persists until the session ends",
    "Given multiple Local Agent Host sessions are active simultaneously, When opening the model picker in each, Then each shows its own independent set of models from respective agents"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Review LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider.getModels() implementation to ensure it returns complete model list",
      "Verify ISessionsProvider.setModel() is properly called when user selects a model in the picker",
      "Add logging to track model selection state changes for local agent sessions",
      "Investigate if there's a race condition between session type change and model picker rendering"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify contrib/chat/browser/modelPicker.ts correctly handles Local Agent Host provider responses",
      "Check if the ModelPickerActionItem properly renders models from local agent host",
      "Add UI debugging to confirm picker state is being initialized for local sessions",
      "Ensure model selection persistence works across session reloads"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test model picker appears correctly when opening new Local Agent Host session",
      "Verify selected model persists after closing and reopening the same session",
      "Confirm different agents show their own distinct models in the picker",
      "Test edge case: agent host restarts while session is active"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Model picker displays all available models for Local Agent Host sessions",
    "Previous model selections persist correctly across session reloads",
    "All acceptance criteria tests pass",
    "Code reviewed and merged to main branch",
    "No regressions in existing agent host functionality"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Is this issue reproducible consistently across all Local Agent Host configurations?",
    "Does the problem occur when switching between different agents within the same host?",
    "Has this been tested with multiple agent hosts running simultaneously?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The model picker component is working correctly for other providers",
    "Local Agent Host properly exposes models via ISessionsProvider.getModels()",
    "The issue is specific to Local Agent Host and not a general provider problem"
  ],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - LocalAgentHostSessionsProvider",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Model Picker documentation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent host system-prompt customization"
  ]
}
```
